description: Advanced exercise whitelist for adult physique clients. Reduces barbell clustering, expands MCC downgrade paths, adds implement variety (landmine, kettlebell, dumbbell, cable).

1.0.2: Restricted Front Squat to DAY_B only
Restricted Incline Bench to DAY_B only
Restricted Trap Bar Deadlift to DAY_B only
Added implement_type field to all exercises
Added 24 new exercises (landmine, kettlebell, cable, dumbbell, band)
Enhanced MCC downgrade paths (cable rows, band press, etc.)

node_3_policy.rationale: NODE 3 is DCC-legal but requires explicit opt-in to prevent generator escalation

progression_governance.rationale: Generator must not progress multiple exercises in same family on different axes within same week

hinge.rationale: High-eccentric hinge work accumulates rapidly; limit H3 exposure

exercises.name: Front Squat
exercises.notes: Anterior-loaded squat; moved to DAY_B to reduce barbell clustering

exercises.name: Bulgarian Split Squat
exercises.notes: Unilateral stability + quad emphasis; high accumulation risk

exercises.name: Landmine Squat (Counterbalanced)
exercises.notes: Low axial load; excellent quad volume without barbell stress

exercises.name: DB Front-Foot Elevated Split Squat (Shallow)
exercises.notes: Unilateral; safer than Bulgarian; control depth to limit fatigue

exercises.name: Goblet Cyclist Squat (Heels Elevated)
exercises.notes: Quad bias without barbell or unilateral overload

exercises.name: Conventional Deadlift
exercises.notes: Primary posterior chain; high systemic load; DAY_A only

exercises.name: Trap Bar Deadlift
exercises.notes: Reduced shear stress; moved to DAY_B to reduce barbell clustering

exercises.name: Nordic Hamstring Curl
exercises.notes: Advanced eccentric; MCC opt-in for H3; subject to family H-node limits

exercises.name: Dumbbell RDL (Bilateral)
exercises.notes: Hamstring/glute emphasis; lower systemic cost than barbell

exercises.name: Landmine RDL
exercises.notes: Posterior chain volume without barbell axial load

exercises.name: Cable Pull-Through
exercises.notes: Hinge stimulus with minimal soreness; excellent MCC downgrade target

exercises.name: FreakAthlete 45° Back Extension (Neutral)
exercises.notes: Posterior chain volume without hinge density

exercises.name: Incline Bench Press (Barbell)
exercises.notes: Upper pec emphasis; moved to DAY_B to reduce barbell clustering

exercises.name: Landmine Press (Half Kneeling, Bilateral)
exercises.notes: Shoulder-friendly vertical load; keep load modest

exercises.name: Band-Resisted Push-Up (Long Bands)
exercises.notes: Excellent MCC downgrade target; variable resistance

exercises.name: Barbell Row (Bent Over)
exercises.notes: Primary horizontal pull; posterior chain isometric

exercises.name: Cable Lat Pulldown (Neutral / Close Grip)
exercises.notes: Neutral grip variation; better elbow health

exercises.name: Cable Low Row (Neutral or Single-Arm)
exercises.notes: Variable grip; excellent barbell row downgrade

exercises.name: Chest-Supported Dumbbell Row (Incline Bench)
exercises.notes: Supported horizontal pull; reduces lower back demand

exercises.name: Suitcase Carry
exercises.notes: Unilateral; anti-lateral flexion; suitable for DAY_D at BAND 1

exercises.name: Goblet Carry
exercises.notes: Anterior load; thoracic extension demand; DAY_D safe at BAND 0-1

exercises.name: Landmine Suitcase Carry
exercises.notes: Frontal plane stability; lower systemic cost

exercises.name: Hip Abduction (Cable/Band)
exercises.notes: Glute medius isolation; frontal plane hip stability

exercises.name: Clamshell (Band)
exercises.notes: Hip external rotation; glute medius activation

exercises.name: Hip External Rotation (Seated)
exercises.notes: Deep hip rotator activation; seated position reduces load

exercises.name: Copenhagen Plank
exercises.notes: Adductor isometric; advanced stability; high accumulation risk

exercises.name: Cable Anti-Rotation Press (Tall / Half Kneeling)
exercises.notes: Progressive anti-rotation load; excellent control variant

exercises.name: Hanging Knee Raise / Bent-Knee Leg Raise
exercises.notes: Anti-flexion isometric; suspended position reduces lower back stress

DAY_A.rationale: Heavy compounds only; limited variety needed; focus on primary lifts

DAY_B.rationale: Primary hypertrophy driver; highest exercise variety; permits H3 techniques

DAY_C.rationale: Capacity + corrective work; moderate variety; stability emphasis

DAY_D.rationale: Recovery focus; minimal variety; movement quality over quantity
